name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would also authorize $5 bil for schools & libraries to provide internet services for students & teachers by providing laptops, wi-fi hotspots, & more, would provide student debt relief & puts in place a temporary suspension of payments for all federal student loan borrowers.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This funding is flexible and can be used to address the unique needs of different communities and can support purchasing technology, providing counseling and mental health services for students and staff, and authorized activities under IDEA and ESEA.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: COVID-19 has driven home that our school systems are not equal and that not everyone has the ability to carry on their education remotely. The Heroes Act begins to address this disparity by providing $90 billion for a State Fiscal Stabilization Fund.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Last but certainly not least, here are some of the education provisions in the Heroes Act:

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act would provide $500 billion directly to state governments and $375 billion for local governments so that we can protect those on the frontlines and ensure that the virus does not decimate public services.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: New Jersey has been hit hard by COVID-19 and our local governments are in desperate need of assistance to pay vital workers such as health care workers, police, firefighters, transportation workers, EMS, and teachers.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would strengthen the Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) to ensure that underserved communities are not overlooked & improves PPP forgiveness by extending covered period. It would provide another $10 billion for EIDL grants including cash advances that don't need to be paid back

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Our neighborhood mom and pop shops are the backbone of our communities and the economic toll of COVID-19 is putting many of them at risk. The Heroes Act would build upon the programs put in place in previous stimulus packages to help small businesses ride out the storm.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: We are still debating the Heroes Act so let's review some provisions for small businesses, states, and municipalities:

name: Albio Sires
tweet: $7 billion would be set aside for Child Care and Development Block Grants and another $850 million would go to the Social Services Block Grant to fund child and family care for essential workers.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Also included is emergency funding for school meal programs and the Child and Adult Care Food Program as well as support for services such as home-delivered meals.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act would boost SNAP benefits, providing $10 billion for the program, as well as an addition $150 million for The Emergency Food Assistance Program (TEFAP) to help local food banks meet the dramatically increased need.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would also dedicate $4 billion to public housing agencies to respond to the coronavirus and protect the 2.2 million families in current housing and any families newly impacted by the pandemic, including those fleeing domestic violence.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would extend the eviction and foreclosure moratorium that was created in the CARES Act, including all renters and homeowners.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would also create a $75 billion Homeowner Assistance Fund to help cover mortgage payments, property taxes, insurance, utilities, and other costs.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act would create a $100 billion Emergency Rental Assistance Program that will help renters cover the costs of their utility bills and rent.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The House is in the process of debating the Heroes Act so next I'll go over housing, nutritional, and child provisions:

name: Albio Sires
tweet: I am also pleased that these stimulus payments would be extended to Individual Taxpayer ID Number (ITIN) holders – a massive oversight in previous stimulus packages that left out so many who contribute so much to our economy and society.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: While I still believe that we need recurring payments to ensure the financial security of families across the country I am pleased that families making less than $150,000 and individuals making less than $75,000 per year will get some assistance.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This is more than the stimulus payments in the CARES Act which was $1,200 per adult and $500 per child. Each eligible family will be able to receive up to $6,000.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This bill does not include recurring payments to individuals which I have been pushing for and will continue to push for. However, it does include another round of stimulus payments of $1,200 per family member.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: $2 billion is set aside for worker training, and almost $1 billion is dedicated to helping process unemployment claims, funding that is much needed as states deal with record levels of claims.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would also extend the $600 a week, supplemental federal unemployment payments through January 31, 2020 – providing some financial relief during this time of uncertainty.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act includes strong workplace standards and would require OSHA to issue strong, enforceable standards for infection control and prohibits employers from retaliating against workers who report infection control issues.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Next up, provisions that cover workers, the unemployed, and individuals:

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act would provide an additional $100 billion for supporting hospitals and frontline care providers and strengthen Medicaid programs across the country.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: New Jerseyans know just how devastating this virus is. We have lost so many of our friends, family, neighbors, and colleagues. Those who have gotten sick shouldn't have to worry about the $ of treatment which is why the Heroes Act ensures that every ppl can access free treatment.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: We need a national strategy on testing with a clear timeline. The Heroes Act requires the Administration to submit such a plan to Congress by June 15th so that we can move forward as a nation in our fight against COVID-19.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It would also allocate an additional $75 billion for testing, contact tracing, and isolation measures. We won’t know the true scale of the health crisis until we have universal testing and to achieve that states and localities will need federal assistance.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Heroes Act would establish a $200 billion Heroes’ Fund to provide hazard pay to the essential workers who are risking their lives each day during the pandemic.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: While it is not perfect, the bill includes hundreds of provisions that will provide relief, so I’ll be highlighting some of them throughout the day. First up are provisions for health care workers, systems, and our response to COVID-19:

tweet: Today, the House of Representatives is considering a 5th stimulus package in response to the COVID-19 pandemic – the Heroes Act. It is comprehensive, addressing the needs of numerous communities including those who have not received the relief they need in previous packages.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Legislating is often a long and frustrating process but my Democratic colleagues and I will continue working on behalf of the American people. It is no accident that this year's NDAA is progressive, and I'll keep working in Congress to advance our priorities through legislation.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The resolution is called H.Con.Res.83, and it directs the President to terminate the use of America’s Armed Forces engaged in hostilities in or against Iran. Just as I supported the NDAA provision, I voted for this important resolution.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: For example, although the House-passed NDAA included a provision I voted for prohibiting unauthorized war with Iran, the final version of the bill did not. But yesterday, the House voted on a similar War Powers resolution regarding the issue.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Legislating is a process of negotiation and compromise, so not every position we want will be included in a final bill. But our system is designed to allow legislative efforts to evolve – just because a policy isn’t in one bill doesn’t mean its doomed.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Legislating is a process of negotiation and compromise, so not every position we want will be included in a final bill. But our system is designed to allow legislative efforts to evolve – just because a policy isn’t in one bill doesn’t mean its doomed.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: And this NDAA authorizes millions of dollars for military climate readiness and resiliency projects, directs DOD to plan for current climate-change vulnerabilities on our mil bases, and authorizes funding for military clean-energy research projects.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The bill also bans the use of PFAS forever-chemicals in firefighting agents used on military bases and in the packaging used for servicemembers’ field meals, known as MREs. This is an important step forward in limiting the exposure of PFAS chems in our food and water supplies.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: And it repeals the “Widow’s Tax,” which is what happens when a fallen servicemember’s surviving spouse – who is entitled to two gov. annuity payments – has one payment reduced dollar-for-dollar by the other. Surviving spouses depend on these payments. I’m proud to vote to fix it.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: For example – and for the first time ever – this year’s NDAA implements 12 weeks of paid parental leave for all federal employees. This leave policy includes leave for birth, adoption, or fostering.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: But this year’s NDAA is among the most progressive ever enacted thanks to House Democrats prioritizing medical, family leave, environmental, and health provisions.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The NDAA is a massive bill (this year’s was over 1,000 pages long) that includes authorization and funding-level agreements for nearly every program run by the Department of Defense (DOD) – this includes servicemember pay, funding for arms upgrades, and so on.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Each year, Congress gets together to debate which national defense programs to reauthorize and which ones to reel in. This is done through a legislative vehicle known as the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA).

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This bill is an important step towards preventing contamination of our food and water sources by a possibly-harmful chemical. It passed the House by a vote of 247 to 159 and now goes to the Senate.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Today, I voted for H.R. 535, the PFAS Action Act, to have the Environmental Protection Agency list PFAS chemicals as hazardous substances in order to strengthen scientific scrutiny of their possible impacts on our health.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: I want to thank you for taking the time to read today’s #FridayFocus on an extremely important topic.  If you have any questions or want more information on immigration legislation or data, please don’t hesitate to reach out to my office.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Debate on immigration policy frequently becomes heated but I ask that you keep the information I shared today in mind.  Our country has provided countless immigrants with an opportunity for a better life and our communities have benefited as a result.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: H.R. 6 passed in the House of Representatives earlier this month and has not yet been scheduled for a vote in the Senate.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: H.R. 6 doesn’t allow any of these people to “cut the line” and receive their citizenship on some sort of fast track, the process would take years. It simply rectifies a horrible mistake within our broken immigration system that left so many people in an uncertain state.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Jersey City is the home of 3,800 H.R. 6 protected immigrants, on avg the Dreamers arrived when they were 9 years old & the avg TPS recipient has been in here since 1995. These immigrants pay $48.6 million in Federal taxes and $23.7 million in State & local taxes each year.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Newark is home to 4,900 H.R. 6 protected immigrants.  On average the Dreamers arrived when they were 9 and most TPS recipients came to Newark in 2000. They pay $22.2 million in Federal taxes and $15.2 million in State and local taxes every year.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Elizabeth is home to 5,400 H.R. 6 protected immigrants, on average the Dreamers arrived at the age of 9 and TPS recipients came in 1997. These 5,400 people pay $35.4 million in Federal taxes and $21.3 million in State and local taxes every year.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Out of all the members on the House Budget Committee, I represent the district with the most individuals (19,500) covered by these programs and we would be severely impacted if the protections were removed.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This is why I am proud to be an original cosponsor of H.R. 6, the American Dream and Promise Act of 2019. This legislation would help to create a pathway to legal permanent resident status for Dreamers and TPS recipients.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: We can argue about what immigration reform should look like, but we can’t deny that there are good people who would get “in line” if there was a line for them at all and that our immigration system has failed them.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Both groups contribute to our economy, work hard to improve their communities, and are Americans in their hearts.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Within our flourishing immigrant population, there is a subset that faces even more obstacles, Dreamers & TPS recipients.  Dreamers were brought to this country as kids & only know America as home while some TPS recipients have been here for decades after fleeing violence & chaos

name: Albio Sires
tweet: These tax dollars fund infrastructure improvements, better school systems, Social Security, and countless other initiatives.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Beyond creating jobs, immigrants in the 8th District pay over $3.3 billion in taxes every year. This money goes to fund programs that positively impact our communities, and millions of Americans across the country.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: If you walk down the street, chances are you’ll pass at least one business that was created by an immigrant or pass a neighbor who works at one.  The 8th District is home to nearly 12,000 of immigrant entrepreneurs.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Despite being less than 14% of the total population, immigrants make up about 30% of all new entrepreneurs.  We see the hard work of these entrepreneurs every day in the 8th District.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Stabilizing populations allow communities to attract businesses, create jobs, employ individuals and boost local government revenues - allowing everyone to benefit from increased investment.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: In fact, the working-age population is only expected to grow by .3 percent each year for the next decade but immigrants will make up 85% of that growth.  This growth also prevents population decline in rural areas, which helps stabilize local populations.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This leads to lower productivity, an erosion of business dynamism, and a general decrease to our economic growth. Immigrants help make up for this shortfall of workers when they join the workforce.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Beyond the links between immigration and lower crime rates, immigration helps make our nation more productive.  Our workforce is aging and as baby boomers retire and birth rates fall, our working-age population shrinks.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: According to one study by the @NewAmericanEcon , 9 out of the 10 American cities that received the largest influx of refugees between 2006 and 2015 saw violent crime rates drop over this same period.  https://t.co/mYZHnp1B7u

name: Albio Sires
tweet: As I said on Wednesday, I just don’t buy the claim that immigrants are criminals and the facts back me up.  On average, cities with substantial immigrant populations have lower crime rates than those with small immigrant populations.

name: Albio Sires

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Thank you for joining me for this #FridayFocus to discuss how the appropriations process works and the importance of the process to our democratic system.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The appropriations process is an important aspect of Congress’s Constitutional power of the purse. Funding levels determined during the appropriations process are crucial to the performance of agencies and implementation of public programs.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Upon both chambers adopting an identical spending bill, the bill is then sent to the President for his signature or veto.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Both chambers must pass an identical bill before it can be signed into law. If there are differences the chambers can then continue to pass amendments to each other until issues are resolved or can hold a conference of members in order to get an agreement.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Once the spending bill passes the House, it is sent to the Senate for consideration. The Senate can also make changes to the bill in the form of amendments. These amendments usually become the topic of debate between the two chambers.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: When a minibus like this comes to the House Floor members are given the chance to offer amendments and change funding levels of the bill before it receives a vote.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The debate this week centered on a minibus, a package of 4 smaller appropriations bills. This minibus, H.R. 2740, considered spending limits for Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, Defense, State and Foreign Operations, and Energy and Water Development.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Following passage in the Appropriations Committee the spending bills will be brought to the House floor for a vote. The spending packages can be voted on individually, all-together, or in smaller groupings known as a “minibus”.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Each subcommittee will then mark up their spending proposal before sending it to the full committee for consideration.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Next, each subcommittee will create a budget that allocates their total predetermined funding to the programs and agencies under their jurisdiction. This power of Congress to determine spending levels is vital for enforcing checks and balances.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Some of the letters I signed supported funding for medical research, affordable housing programs, community health centers, community development grants, law enforcement grants, and gun violence prevention measures.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Once these letters are circulated, members formally submit their funding priorities to the 12 subcommittees.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The appropriations process begins in the House, it is important the proposed funding levels reflect the needs of constituents, not just the White House’s desired budget.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Members lead & sign onto letters to the Appropriations Committee outlining requests important to their district.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The appropriations process began a few months ago. After reviewing the President’s proposed budget, Members of Congress have an opportunity to submit funding requests to support various programs, agencies, and priorities.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This week, the House of Representatives started debating amendments on our first appropriations package for FY 2020. For this week’s #FridayFocus let’s take a deeper look into how the appropriations process works.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Thank you for joining this #FridayFocus on the President’s budget and how Congress starts its appropriations process.  Stay tuned for future updates as we continue crafting legislation that will fund the government in fiscal year 2020.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Congress holds the power of the purse and it is important that we craft a thoughtful budget that takes into account the needs of Americans – not just the desires of the President.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: As we continue on the appropriations process I’ll be sharing more info about the programs I support and why.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: To name just a few – I’m supporting funding for medical research, affordable housing programs, Community  Development Block Grants, community health centers, law enforcement grants, and gun violence prevention measures.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: I’ve signed letters supporting numerous programs that make a real difference in communities across the country.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Deadlines are set for when these letters of support are then sent to one of the 12 subcommittees with specific jurisdiction over these programs, where they are reviewed by appropriators and used to create a final budget resolution.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: These letters outline recommended funding levels for programs important to our constituents.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: This is where the appropriations process comes in.  Congress appropriates funding to different departments and each member can circulate and sign letters of support for programs, agencies, or priorities.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: As I mentioned, along with oversight we also play a role in the crafting of a budget resolution and the eventual bills that set funding levels for the next fiscal year.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It is important that we have the opportunity to hear from these officials because, although the Executive Branch does not have the power of the purse, they are in charge of overseeing the execution of the programs that Congress funds and it is important to get their insight.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: As a newly appointed member of the @budgetdems it is now my responsibility to question officials on funding levels and the Committee has had three administration members come in to discuss the sharp cuts to numerous non-defense programs.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Committee will convene regularly to receive updates on the direction of our government’s spending and how our annual budget can best reflect the priorities of our nation.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: One of the Committee’s main responsibilities is to carry out oversight on budget proposals that are presented to Congress.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 created the Budget Committee to allow Congress to develop a self-governing group of members to analyze the President’s budget proposal and unite it with congressional plans.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: It’s important to note, though, that these are just suggestions. The budget is what the president *wants* programs/agencies/departments to be funded at – Congress reviews the wish list and changes it to reflect *its* own priorities.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: So, for example, if a president thinks affordable housing or the Special Olympics are good, positive things that the government should get behind, his budget proposal will include funding for the departments that oversee those programs.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: So, each year the President sends his proposed budget – what he would ideally like Congress to do – for members of Congress to review.   A budget is like a statement of principles – a president’s budget will mirror a president's values.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Everything from the Department of Defense to the National Institutes of Health fall under the Executive Branch and each year funding levels must be set. However, the President does not have the power of the purse – the Constitution explicitly gives that authority to Congress.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The President’s budget is a (very large) document outlining the Administration’s policy priorities and how they ought to be funded. The document itself is very large because the Executive Branch oversees massive government agencies.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Overall, the federal budget process is straightforward: in the spring, the president proposes a budget, Congress reviews that budget, and then Congress tries to agree on a final budget resolution.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: On March 11, 2019, the President released his budget proposal for the next fiscal year.  This budget goes to Congress and is available to the public as a statement of the administration’s priorities.  For today’s #FridayFocus let’s look at how the budget process actually works.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Today’s tour from DC to NYC highlighted once again that rail infrastructure is key to transporting people and goods. It is only a piece of the puzzle that keeps our nation’s transportation systems running smoothly but if one piece is missing, the whole thing suffers.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Today was just a brief overview of the NEC’s infrastructure struggle. Old bridges and tunnels don’t get fixed by themselves, and states need federal help to finance these upgrades.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Specifically, if one of the 2 tunnels fail, rail capacity on this route between NYC and NJ would drop by 75%. That would cripple the economy, the housing market, and job prospects in the region. This doomsday scenario would take years, if not decades, to recover from.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Gateway Project would repair New Jersey and New York’s share of this infra. It would take some time and be expensive, but it’s worth it. If this any part of this infrastructure fails, in Jersey or in Maryland or anywhere else along the Corridor, it’d be bad for all of us.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Here’s a close up look at the state of these 100+ year old tunnels.  https://t.co/0lNGFttmiw

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Again, though, decades of nonstop use will degrade anything, and Superstorm Sandy made things worse. I’m 2012, the storm flooded these tunnels, and the saltwater did some terrible damage.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: So you have an idea of how important it is — the tunnel hosts 450 trains each day, carrying about 200k commuters between Newark and Manhattan. Basically, this is the busiest rail corridor in the Western Hemisphere.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: We all know about the Hudson tunnel and it’s challenges. It’s bad enough that this is another piece of infrastructure that’s over a century old, but this also happens to be *the* linchpin of the NE Corridor.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Portal Bridge is over a century old and is used by over 400 trains each day. It is a major source of delays and there are frequent electrical and mechanical issues. A clear sign that repair/replacement is long overdue.  https://t.co/hBpaafxhkD

name: Albio Sires
tweet: The Gateway Project is the plan to address a lot of these problems and is broken into a series of smaller goals including work on the Portal North Bridge and the Hudson Tunnels.

name: Albio Sires
tweet: Unfortunately, as we’ve shown throughout the morning, a lot of the infrastructure supporting the movement of millions is in desperate need of repair and replacement.
